title: Coelogyne tortilis
intro: Coelogyne tortilis is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its long-lasting, white to yellowish flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate respiratory issues and support immune function. Research has identified bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelogyne tortilis is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies for respiratory and immune system support.
taxonomy: Coelogyne tortilis is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family encompasses the largest and most diverse group of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Coelogyne tortilis is a genus of orchids native to Southeast Asia. The genus Coelogyne is typically distinguished by its racemose inflorescences and sympodial growth habit. The classification of Coelogyne tortilis also places it within the subtribe Coelogyneinae.
